purpose: Compare the original recursive shared-prefix scorer against the EXP171 batched same-depth shared-prefix implementation on item-incoterms without training.
conclusion: The batched shared-prefix implementation is not score-exact relative to the recursive scorer: the prefix/final score surfaces differ by up to roughly 0.069 log-score on the audited item-incoterms batches. The audited batches did not show top1 or top5 ordering mismatches, so this does not by itself prove ranking-path collapse, but it is enough to classify EXP171 as a non-exact implementation-path rewrite rather than a clean rejection of the original EXP108 shared-prefix information path.
search_space_consequence: Do not relaunch EXP171 or tune batching/chunk sizes. Any future shared-prefix implementation must first be score-equivalent or explicitly justify the changed score surface while preserving throughput. Otherwise continue with different paper- or prior-ablation-backed scalable Stage4-compatible candidate-aware autocomplete scorers.
